memories.memory_text: The sword felt heavier than I expected when Yu placed it in my hands, and I remember thinking how strange it was that something so thin could weigh so much—then seeing the intricate scabbard and whispering, 'Exquisite!' before asking who it really belonged to.
grounded_facts: Yu placed the sword in Jen's hands.
Jen commented on the sword's weight and the beauty of its scabbard.
Jen said 'Exquisite!' and asked about the sword's owner.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying out loud that it must be exciting to be a fighter, truly free—then the silence after admitting I haven't lived the life I want, not with the wedding coming, and how Yu just looked at me like he saw right through it.
grounded_facts: Jen expressed longing for the freedom of a fighter's life.
Jen stated she hasn't lived the life she wants due to her upcoming marriage.
Yu responded with silence and a studied look after Jen's admission.

memories.memory_text: I wrote Yu's name in bold strokes, just for fun, and she said it looked like 'sword'—I froze, but didn't say a word.
grounded_facts: Jen wrote Yu's name during a calligraphy session.
Yu observed that her name resembled the character for 'sword'.
Jen reacted with a slight freeze after Yu's comment.
Jen associated calligraphy with fencing, though she denied knowing the connection.

memories.memory_text: I told Yu I wished I were like the heroes in the books—free to choose whom I love—because marrying the Gou boy isn't true happiness, not really.
grounded_facts: Jen expressed a wish to be like the heroes in books who are free to choose their love.
Jen stated that marrying the Gou boy is not true happiness.
Jen acknowledged her upcoming marriage is arranged by her parents for her father's career.

memories.memory_text: I felt the dry air pulling through my hair, rough and warm, like it was stripping away something heavy I didn’t know I was carrying.
grounded_facts: Jen is in a moving carriage traveling through the Mongolian desert.
She allows the dry desert air to flow through her hair.
The sensation of the air is physically noticeable and distinct.

memories.memory_text: I kept staring at the mountainside—its edges sharp against the sky—while my mother’s voice buzzed somewhere far off, like wind through a crack.
grounded_facts: Jen is looking out the side of the carriage at a beautiful mountainside.
Her mother is speaking to her, asking if she is listening, but Jen does not respond or turn away.
Her attention remains fixed outside, ignoring her mother's complaints.

memories.memory_text: I remember the cold night air on my face when Li lifted my mask, his voice calm like he already knew me—he offered to teach me, said I could be his disciple. I spat back that Wudan was a whorehouse and ran, but his words stuck, heavy in my chest.
grounded_facts: Li confronted the figure (Jen) in the monastery garden at night.
Li evaded the figure's attacks and used a branch to repel her.
Li told the figure she needed restraint and offered to teach her to fight with the Green Destiny.
Li said he wanted a disciple worthy of Wudan's secrets and was willing to risk her turning against him.
The figure asked 'Why do you want to teach me?' and later rejected the offer, calling Wudan a whorehouse before fleeing.

memories.memory_text: I remember grabbing the lance—cold metal in my hand—from that dead guard’s body, right after Lo took my comb. The horse was still kicking, and I just knew I couldn’t stay in that carriage.
grounded_facts: Jen retrieves a lance from the body of a dead guard immediately after being robbed by Lo.
Jen exits the carriage before picking up the lance.
The dead guard's horse is flailing beside the body when Jen takes the weapon.
Lo and the bandits are present and preparing to flee on horseback as Jen picks up the lance.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed one of the arrows, tried to raise it against him—but my legs gave out and I fell. I couldn't even stand, let alone fight.
grounded_facts: Jen quickly grabs an arrow upon waking.
She attempts to attack with the arrow but collapses due to physical weakness.
Her legs fail her, preventing her from maintaining a threatening stance.

memories.memory_text: He handed me food and milk. I took it slow at first, not trusting him—but then I couldn't stop myself. I ate it fast, desperate, like I hadn't eaten in days.
grounded_facts: Lo pours horse milk from a sac and hands Jen a chunk of food.
Jen initially accepts the food grudgingly.
She then eats it hungrily, unable to control her pace due to overwhelming hunger.

memories.memory_text: I told him not to ever come back, my voice shaking through tears—he just stood there at the window, silent.
grounded_facts: Jen whispered 'Don't ever come back' to Lo while crying.
Lo remained at the window after being told to leave.
A servant approached the door during this moment, prompting Jen to urge Lo to go.

memories.memory_text: After he jumped out, I opened my hand and saw the comb—cold and familiar—suddenly back in my palm, though I didn’t remember taking it.
grounded_facts: Lo placed a small object (the jade comb) in Jen's hand before leaving through the window.
Jen discovered the comb in her palm after the maid left.
She gasped upon recognizing it, indicating surprise and emotional recognition.

memories.memory_text: I can't see anything—just the dark red behind my eyes, the weight of the cloth pressing down, and the sway of the carriage carrying me somewhere I don't want to go.
grounded_facts: Jen's head is covered with red cloth.
Jen is inside a moving bride's carriage.
Jen is not speaking.

memories.memory_text: I felt the arrow punch into the wood beside me, quiet and sharp—then I just pulled it out, like pulling a splinter. The red cloth slipped from my face, but I didn’t hear anything after that.
grounded_facts: An arrow entered the wedding carriage and embedded in the interior panelling.
Jen removed the arrow herself while lifting the red veil from her face.
Lo's voice is heard from outside the carriage, but the script does not confirm Jen reacted to or acknowledged it.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed the waitress’s arm and told her the cup was dirty—she flinched like I’d burned her.
grounded_facts: Jen physically stopped the waitress by grabbing her arm.
Jen stated 'The cup is dirty' in a firm, rejecting tone.
The waitress reacted with fluster and removed the cup.

memories.memory_text: They drew their weapons, but I cut them to scraps—then said, 'He is my defeated foe!'
grounded_facts: Two men approached Jen and asked her name and destination.
Jen responded with 'Long' and 'Anywhere there's action.'
The men drew weapons after being dismissed.
Jen destroyed their weapons, reducing them to scraps.
One man asked if Jen was related to Li Mu Bai.
Jen replied, 'He is my defeated foe!'

memories.memory_text: I was just sitting, sipping tea, when that Iron Arm Mi charged at me—didn’t even give me time to finish my meal. One poke to his pressure point and he went down like a sack of rice.
grounded_facts: Jen was seated and drinking tea when Mi attacked.
Jen defeated Mi with a single pressure point strike while remaining seated.
Mi introduced himself as 'Iron Arm Mi' and claimed to seek a lesson.

memories.memory_text: After they all came at me, I drew the Green Destiny and shouted who I really am—Invincible Sword Goddess. I don’t know if they heard me over the slashing, but I meant every word.
grounded_facts: Jen unsheathed the Green Destiny and engaged multiple opponents.
Jen declared herself the 'Invincible Sword Goddess' during the fight.
She delivered a monologue while slashing through the attackers.
The declaration included references to Li Mu Bai, Southern Crane, and Wudan Mountain.

memories.memory_text: I stood at the bottom of the hall, my voice coming out quiet but clear—'Sister Shu Lien...' I saw her turn, the incense smoke curling behind her, and for a moment, I wasn't sure if she'd even recognize me.
grounded_facts: Jen speaks the words 'Sister Shu Lien...' upon seeing Yu (Shu Lien) in the Hall of Ancestral Worship.
Jen is dishevelled and positioned downstairs in the hall when she is seen by Yu.
Yu is lighting incense for her former fiancé Meng when Jen arrives.

memories.memory_text: I broke down on Yu's shoulder, trembling—I couldn't hold it in anymore. All I managed was 'You, sister...' before the weight of it crushed me.
grounded_facts: Jen breaks down in tears while on Yu's bed.
Jen initiates physical contact by leaning on Yu's shoulder.
Jen speaks only two words before being overwhelmed emotionally.

memories.memory_text: I told her I didn't care—she was never a real friend. When she blocked my way and shouted 'Put the sword down!', I jumped out to escape.
grounded_facts: Jen verbally rejects Yu's loyalty by calling her not a real friend.
Yu intercepts Jen physically before she leaves.
Yu demands Jen put the sword down.
Jen escapes by jumping out of the room after the confrontation.

memories.memory_text: I saw Yu hesitate, the broken blade at my neck—then pull back. I didn't wait. I slashed her arm. Hers now.
grounded_facts: Yu paused during combat, holding a broken blade near Jen's neck but chose not to strike.
Jen took advantage of that pause to slice Yu's arm.
Jen handed or gestured the sword toward Yu after wounding her, saying 'Take it!'

memories.memory_text: Li Mu Bai jumped in, yelling I didn't deserve the sword. I told him no more lectures—then challenged him. Let the blade decide.
grounded_facts: Li Mu Bai intervened immediately after Jen wounded Yu, expressing outrage.
Li stated Jen did not deserve the Green Destiny.
Jen responded with 'Not another lecture! On guard!' and initiated a duel by leaping to the rooftop.

memories.memory_text: I was running through the bamboo, blood on my clothes, every gust of wind feeling like Li was right behind me—I could feel him watching, waiting.
grounded_facts: Jen runs through the bamboo forest, stained with blood and mud.
She perceives the wind as sounding like an air attack by Li.
She feels pursued and on edge, sensing Li's presence even when not in sight.

memories.memory_text: I stood on the same bending bamboo as Li, trying to throw him off, and when he asked to see the real me, I spat back—what does he know about a true heart?
grounded_facts: Jen and Li land on the same bamboo stalk, which bends under their weight.
Jen attempts to shake Li off the bamboo.
She responds to Li's statement with the question, 'What do you know about a true heart?'

memories.memory_text: I jumped onto the rock and told Li if he could take the sword in three moves, I’d go with him—but he disarmed me too fast. When I demanded he give it back, he just said I had no use for the sword and threw it into the rapids.
grounded_facts: Jen confronts Li on a narrow rock in the rapids.
Jen challenges Li to take the sword in three moves.
Li disarms Jen and throws the Green Destiny into the river.
Jen hears Li say 'Then you have no use for the sword' before the sword is thrown.

memories.memory_text: I dove after the sword and managed to grab it, but the current pulled me under—I couldn’t breathe, couldn’t fight it. The last thing I remember is Jade Fox’s hand yanking me out before everything went black.
grounded_facts: Jen dives into the rapids to retrieve the sword.
Jen recovers the sword but is overwhelmed by the current.
Jen begins drowning and loses consciousness.
Jade Fox rescues Jen from the water.

memories.memory_text: I remember the blue smoke from the needle, the smell of burning paste and incense—Fox’s voice cutting through the haze, telling me we’d never go back, that we were all each other had. I couldn’t move, couldn’t speak, just held the Green Destiny as she left me there.
grounded_facts: Fox heated medicine paste and burned a needle, producing blue smoke.
Incense was lit and placed near Jen.
Fox spoke about not returning to Peking, rejecting Jen's family, and affirming their bond.
Jen was too weak to respond and was left clutching the Green Destiny.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying the words—'She used Purple Yin... Purple Yin poison. It goes straight to the heart.' My voice was weak, but clear. I knew it was true the moment I said it.
grounded_facts: Jen states that Jade Fox used Purple Yin poison.
Jen explains that the poison goes straight to the heart.
This information is delivered after Jade Fox's death and before Li confirms he is poisoned.

memories.memory_text: I took the comb, mumbled 'Spare your energy. I'll be back!' and limped away. I could feel the weight of it in my hand—the comb, the promise, the only thing keeping me moving.
grounded_facts: Yu gives Jen a comb to deliver to Mrs. Wu in exchange for help.
Jen responds with 'Spare your energy. I'll be back!' before leaving.
Jen is physically impaired and must move with difficulty.

memories.memory_text: I pushed through the guards and asked for Mrs. Wu—then showed Yu's comb like Shu Lien said. I remember the weight of it in my hand, cold and solid, the only thing that got me through that gate.
grounded_facts: Jen pushes past the guards at the Yuan compound.
Jen asks, 'Where is Mrs. Wu?'
Jen produces Yu's comb, instructed by Shu Lien to show it.
Aunt Wu recognizes the comb and allows Jen entry.

memories.memory_text: I saw Yu holding Li’s body, the sun shining on them, and when she let go of his hand, I knew he was gone. I couldn’t move—just stood at the door, whispering it like I needed to hear it myself.
grounded_facts: Jen observes Yu cradling Li's dead body and releasing his stiff fingers.
The sun is shining into the factory, illuminating the scene.
Jen is positioned at the door and asks, 'He's gone?'

memories.memory_text: Yu told me to go to Wudan Mountain. Lo was waiting for me there. She told me to be true to myself.
grounded_facts: Yu speaks directly to Jen, instructing her to go to Wudan Mountain.
Yu mentions Lo is waiting for Jen there.
Yu tells Jen to be true to herself.

memories.memory_text: I saw Lo at the gate—then he was running toward me. I said hello, my voice low, maybe trembling, but I stood my ground despite the pain in my leg.
grounded_facts: Jen arrives at Wu Tan Temple gate after climbing up to it.
Lo is present at the gate and runs toward Jen.
Jen greets Lo upon his approach.

memories.memory_text: I remember Lo’s hands on me, warm and careful, like I might break—but I didn’t. I stayed whole.
grounded_facts: Jen and Lo engaged in a physical intimate act.
The interaction was consensual and emotionally significant.
Jen remained physically present and responsive during the act.

memories.memory_text: I stood on the bridge, the clouds below me thick and endless, and told Lo to make a wish—then I turned and leapt, and the sky opened up like it was waiting.
grounded_facts: Jen was on a bridge over cloud-covered gorges
Jen spoke the words 'Make a wish, Lo' before leaping
Jen saw Lo close his eyes and smile before she jumped
Jen disappeared into the clouds after leaping

memories.scene_title: 35、EXT. STREET OUTSIDE GOVERNOR YU'S COMPOUND - NIGHT
memories.memory_text: I saw Tsai and May drop from the tree where they’d been watching Jen and slip away into the night—silent, like shadows breaking loose.
grounded_facts: Tsai and May were hidden in a tree, observing Jen.
They dropped down silently and walked away.
Lo observed this from another rooftop.

memories.memory_text: I slipped through the window and she startled awake, her sword already at my chest—then it clattered down when she saw me. I grabbed her wrist and said what I came to say: I was wrong to let her go.
grounded_facts: Lo entered Jen's room at night through the window.
Jen woke up and pointed a sword at Lo, then dropped it upon recognizing him.
Lo grabbed Jen by the wrist.
Lo admitted he was wrong to let Jen go and urged her to come back with him.

memories.memory_text: I remember shouting 'Don't touch the women!' as we hit the caravan—arrows flying, guards screaming. I meant it. They were under my protection now, not theirs.
grounded_facts: Lo shouted 'Don't touch the women!' during the raid on the caravan.
The command was issued in the presence of armed bandits and captives.
Madam Yu fainted after hearing the command, though unharmed.

memories.memory_text: I took the comb from her hand—Jen’s—before she could run. I don’t know why I kept it. Maybe to mark the moment I took control, not just of the raid, but of what comes after.
grounded_facts: Lo approached the carriage after the raid and took the comb from Jen's hand.
Jen fled the carriage immediately after the comb was taken.
The comb was seized before Jen picked up the lance and the bandits retreated.

memories.memory_text: I knocked her off the horse when she tried to stab me with the lance—she shouldn't have come at me like that. As I rode ahead, I told her to hurry back to her mother, the comb lying in the dust behind her.
grounded_facts: Lo disarmed Jen by grabbing the lance she used to attack him.
Lo caused Jen to fall off her horse by flinging the lance.
Lo mocked Jen by telling her to 'hurry back to your mother' after disarming her.
The comb was left on the ground after the confrontation.

memories.memory_text: I saw Jen take down one of my men fast—then all of them rushing her at once. I stepped in and said, 'She's mine,' because I knew no one would stop otherwise.
grounded_facts: Jen arrived at the desert bluff while the bandits were dividing loot.
One bandit approached Jen, was quickly incapacitated by her.
Other bandits attacked Jen in response, were defeated.
Lo intervened by declaring 'She's mine' to halt the fight.

memories.memory_text: I told her my name—Lo—and that the Hans call me Dark Cloud. I said it like I always do, trying to sound light, but this time it felt different, like I was giving something real away.
grounded_facts: Lo dismounts and looks at the dry creek bed.
Lo says: 'Well, there used to be! What's your name? I'm Lo. The Hans call me Dark Cloud. I'm not that tall or big, but I'm quick as lightning.'

memories.memory_text: She kicked me—out of nowhere—and then we were fighting, both of us so tired, until I couldn't hold on anymore and just collapsed. I remember the dust hitting my face.
grounded_facts: Jen kicks Lo after his dialogue.
They fight ferociously.
Lo, exhausted, collapses after the fight.

memories.memory_text: I handed Jen the food, and she took it like she didn’t trust me—but then she started eating fast, almost desperate. I told her, 'You're eating too fast. Slowly,' because I could see her body was weak, and if she choked or got sick, it would only make things worse.
grounded_facts: Lo provided Jen with food and horse milk.
Jen initially resisted but began eating quickly due to hunger.
Lo observed Jen's physical weakness and instructed her to eat slowly.

memories.memory_text: I was tending the fire, whistling while the chicken roasted, and then—sudden pain, a flash of Jen with a rock. I didn’t see it coming.
grounded_facts: Lo was present at a fire where a chicken was roasting.
Lo was whistling before being attacked.
Jen appeared suddenly and struck Lo on the head with a rock.
Lo lost consciousness immediately after the blow.

memories.memory_text: I told her to relax, that if I’d wanted to hurt her, I would’ve already done it. I offered her clean water and my clothes, telling her they were clean and that I’d sing so she’d know where I was.
grounded_facts: Lo untied Jen after she woke up tied in the cave.
Lo provided fresh water for Jen to bathe, which he had brought up despite it being hard to get.
Lo offered Jen his clean clothes to wear after the bath.
Lo told Jen he would sing so she would know he was outside and not near the bathing area.

memories.memory_text: I started singing a tribal song as I walked out, loud enough for her to hear—la-la-ing through parts I couldn’t remember—so she’d feel safe knowing I wasn’t lurking nearby.
grounded_facts: Lo began singing a tribal song while walking away from the makeshift bathroom.
His singing was loud and included improvised 'la-la' parts where he forgot the lyrics.
The purpose of the singing was to let Jen know his location and give her privacy.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the cactus needles from her feet, and when she demanded the comb again, I told her I don’t take orders from anyone—then she stabbed me, quick and sudden, just below the chest.
grounded_facts: Lo was removing cactus needles from Jen's feet.
Jen demanded the return of her comb.
Lo refused her demand by stating he doesn’t take orders from anyone.
Jen grabbed an arrow and stabbed Lo, drawing blood from his chest.

memories.memory_text: After she stabbed me, I lunged at her in fury, we fought hard, and then—somehow—being on top of her, the struggle shifted, turned into something else entirely, something burning and raw.
grounded_facts: Lo reacted to the stabbing by lunging at Jen.
They tussled briefly, ending with Lo on top.
The physical struggle transitioned into passionate intimacy.

memories.memory_text: I told Jen about seeing a thousand shooting stars as a boy, how I was alone after my parents were gone, and how I started riding the desert searching for them, still searching.
grounded_facts: Lo mentions being an orphan and seeing a thousand shooting stars as a boy.
He says he used to look for stars alone and believed riding to the end of the desert would help him find them.
He states he has been riding the desert ever since that night.

memories.memory_text: I told her the truth—that being Dark Cloud isn't who I am, just a mask to survive, and that my gang became the family I never had.
grounded_facts: Lo explains that life in the desert requires joining a gang to survive.
He says the gang slowly becomes family.
He admits the 'Dark Cloud' identity is a fabrication to scare others and make life easier.

memories.memory_text: I saw the glint of uniforms through my binoculars—too far to make out faces, but the insignia was clear enough. I told Jen they were coming, but she didn’t say a word.
grounded_facts: Lo sees uniformed men through binoculars
Lo identifies the men as Jen's father's men
Lo speaks to Jen about the approaching men
Jen does not respond

memories.memory_text: I saw the troop of uniformed men riding in the distance and knew they were getting closer—Jen’s father’s men. I told her they were still out there, circling, and it is trouble for me.
grounded_facts: Lo observes uniformed men in the distance from the cliff.
Lo identifies them as Jen's father's men.
Lo states, 'It is trouble for me,' indicating personal risk.

memories.memory_text: I told Jen I wanted her to be mine forever. I spoke of the legend—how God grants a wish to anyone who jumps from the mountain. I said belief makes it possible, and that a faithful heart makes wishes come true.
grounded_facts: Lo expresses his desire for a permanent relationship with Jen.
Lo recounts a legend about jumping from the mountain to have a wish granted by God.
Lo states that belief is important for the wish to happen.
Lo references the elders' saying that a faithful heart makes wishes come true.

memories.memory_text: I felt the cool weight of Jen's jade comb in my palm as she pressed it into my hand, her fingers trembling—her voice breaking as she told me to keep it safe and return it when we're together again.
grounded_facts: Jen removed the jade comb from her hair and gave it to Lo.
Jen asked Lo to keep the comb safe and return it when they are together again.
Lo responded with 'I will,' making a promise.
Jen was crying and then smiled painfully during the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I stood at the window, her words still hanging in the air—'Don't ever come back'—and I had to ask, 'So it's over?' She said yes, and it felt like the last thread snapped.
grounded_facts: Lo asked 'So it's over?' after Jen told him not to come back.
Jen responded with 'Yes,' confirming the end of their relationship.

memories.memory_text: I pressed the comb into her hand before climbing out—no words, just that small thing I’d kept—then dropped into the dark as the door opened behind me.
grounded_facts: Lo gave Jen a small object (later revealed to be a comb) before leaving through the window.
He exited just as the maid opened the door from the hallway.

memories.memory_text: I shouted for Jen through the chaos, my voice raw as I broke into the crowd—'Come with me to the desert! You're mine!'—and for a moment, nothing else existed but her name on my lips and the arrows flying where I knew they’d miss.
grounded_facts: Lo shouted 'Jen! Come with me! You're mine!' and 'Come with me to the desert! Jen! Come with me to Xin Jiang!' from outside the wedding carriage.
Lo ran wildly into the crowd during Jen's wedding procession.
An arrow was shot into the carriage before Lo's appearance, though it is not specified who fired it.

memories.memory_text: I felt Li Mu Bai's fingers press hard into my neck, his voice sharp in my ears—'Where is Jade Fox?' I couldn't move, just stared at him, caught.
grounded_facts: Li Mu Bai physically restrained Lo by pressing fingers against his neck.
Li Mu Bai interrogated Lo, demanding to know Jade Fox's location.
Lo was immobilized during the confrontation.

memories.memory_text: Then Yu came sprinting around the corner, shoved Li away, and grabbed me with a shout—'Come with me! Hurry!'—and I ran, not thinking, just following.
grounded_facts: Yu intervened by pushing Li Mu Bai away from Lo.
Yu directly addressed Lo, urging immediate escape.
Lo complied and fled the scene with Yu.

memories.memory_text: I sat there, hollow, after telling them everything. When Yu asked if I wanted to see her again, something cracked—I said 'All right' and took the plan, though waiting felt like dying.
grounded_facts: Lo was sitting dejectedly after telling his story to Yu and Li.
Yu questioned Lo about wanting to see Jen again.
Li proposed writing an introduction for Lo to take to Wudan, instructing him to wait there for news.
Lo agreed by saying 'All right.'

memories.memory_text: I was sent to Wudan Mountain by Li Mu Bai—far from Jen, though I still think of her under the desert stars.
grounded_facts: Lo was sent to Wudan Mountain by Li Mu Bai.
This placement separates Lo from Jen.
The move is framed as a protective and legitimizing action.

memories.memory_text: I saw her coming up the path, and I ran to her—she was finally back at the gate, standing there like she’d never been gone.
grounded_facts: Lo sees Jen approaching the temple gate.
Lo runs toward Jen upon seeing her.
Jen greets Lo when they meet.

memories.memory_text: I remember the warmth of Jen’s skin against mine, the quiet breaths between us in the temple’s stillness—how for a moment, the world outside ceased to exist.
grounded_facts: Lo and Jen engaged in intimate physical contact in the Wu Tan Temple.
The act occurred in a quiet, enclosed space, suggesting privacy and emotional intensity.
The moment is remembered by Lo as a rare instance of closeness and escape from external threats.

memories.memory_text: I woke up and reached for her, but the space beside me was cold—she was gone. The jade comb lay on the pillow where she’d been, the only thing left behind.
grounded_facts: Lo wakes up and discovers Jen is no longer beside him.
The jade comb is present on the pillow next to Lo after Jen's departure.

memories.memory_text: I closed my eyes and wished to be back in the desert, together again—just her and me beneath the open sky.
grounded_facts: Lo is present on the bridge with Jen.
Lo speaks the line: 'To be back in the desert, together again.'
Lo closes his eyes before speaking the line, indicating a deliberate act of wishing.

memories.memory_text: She smiled, turned, and leapt into the clouds—they caught her like a breath, and then she was gone. I felt the tears before I knew I was smiling.
grounded_facts: Jen turns and leaps into the clouds after Lo makes his wish.
The clouds appear to catch Jen gently before she disappears.
Lo remains on the bridge, smiling with tears rolling down his cheeks.

memories.memory_text: I was sealing the last of the linen bundle when Aunt Wu rushed in, breathless—"Li Mu Bai is here!"—and my hands stilled, the weight of the words pressing like a blade's edge against my ribs.
grounded_facts: Yu was in the process of packing small items in a linen wrapper.
Aunt Wu entered abruptly and announced Li Mu Bai's arrival.
The announcement occurred while Yu was engaged in a quiet, focused task.

memories.memory_text: I asked him why he broke off his meditation, and he said it was because of something he couldn't let go of—his voice trembled, and I felt it too, though I didn’t speak it.
grounded_facts: Yu asked Li Mu Bai why he left meditation early.
Li Mu Bai responded that he broke off meditation because of 'something I can't let go of'.
The exchange occurred in the Yuan Practice Hall, after Yu expressed envy for Li's time at Wudan Mountain.

memories.memory_text: He unwrapped the silk and showed me the Green Destiny Sword—its blade gleamed—and asked me to deliver it to Sir Te.
grounded_facts: Li Mu Bai carried a large object wrapped in silk into the Yuan Practice Hall.
He revealed the Green Destiny Sword by unwrapping it in front of Yu.
He requested that Yu deliver the sword to Sir Te.

memories.memory_text: I stood outside the depot as Boss Giao inspected the herbs we delivered, and when he said I was a credit to my father’s memory, I could only reply, 'Thank you,' though the weight of it settled deep.
grounded_facts: Yu delivered medicinal herbs successfully.
Boss Giao acknowledged Yu's work and linked it to her father's legacy.
Yu responded with 'Thank you' after receiving praise.

memories.memory_text: I placed Li Mu Bai's sword into Sir Te's hands and asked him to help Li leave his past behind, my voice steady but my heart heavy with what I couldn't say.
grounded_facts: Yu presents Li Mu Bai's sword to Sir Te.
Yu speaks directly to Sir Te, requesting that he help Li Mu Bai move beyond his troubles.
Yu refers to the sword as something that has brought Li Mu Bai both trouble and glory.

memories.memory_text: Sir Te said he's always known about my feelings for Li Mu Bai. I didn't confirm it, but I said we weren't cowards.
grounded_facts: Sir Te tells Yu he has known about her feelings for Li Mu Bai for years.
Yu responds by saying she and Li Mu Bai are not cowards.
Yu does not confirm or deny the feelings directly.

memories.memory_text: I placed Li Mu Bai's sword into Jen's hands, feeling the weight of it as I let go—she remarked how heavy it was, and I told her the handle was weighted, the blade no ordinary metal, though still the lightest of weapons for one who knows how to carry it.
grounded_facts: Yu physically handed the sword, still in its sheath, to Jen.
Yu commented on the sword's weight, the heavy handle, and the nature of the blade.
Yu stated that the sword is the lightest of weapons despite its weight, implying familiarity with weapon handling.

memories.memory_text: I took the sword from Sir Te, feeling its balance in my hands, and when he drew it in the dark, the blue glow revealed something ancient—something beyond mere steel.
grounded_facts: Sir Te handed the sword to Governor Yu.
The sword emitted a luminous blue glow when unsheathed in the dark.
Yu closed his eyes to sense the sword's weight.
The sword had physical dimensions and missing rubies noted by Te.

memories.memory_text: His words about the Imperial Court not being the true source of power stayed with me—especially how strength must be supple, and how alliances in the Giang Hu matter more than official rank.
grounded_facts: Sir Te stated that the sword rules nothing by itself.
Sir Te advised Yu to not depend only on the Imperial Court.
Sir Te emphasized using contacts in the Giang Hu underworld to ensure position.
Sir Te described the need to be strong yet supple as the way to rule.

memories.memory_text: I struck first, but the thief deflected my attack with precision—familiar footwork, balanced shift in weight. That’s when it hit me: only Wudan training moves like that.
grounded_facts: Yu attacks the thief and is repelled.
Yu observes the thief's movement and questions their Wudan training.

memories.memory_text: After they leapt away, I chased them across the rooftops and unleashed a storm of blows—fast, unrelenting. I had to regain control before they disappeared into the night.
grounded_facts: The thief leaps to another rooftop.
Yu catches up and resumes attack with a relentless series of quick blows.

memories.memory_text: I felt the shift in the fight—my movements were sharper, his breath ragged. He was mine to take down.
grounded_facts: Yu was gaining the upper hand in the fight against the thief.
The combat was physical and close-range, allowing her to sense the opponent's weakening state.

memories.memory_text: A small arrow came out of the dark. I caught it mid-flight, but when I looked up, the thief was gone. The shaft felt strange in my hand—deliberate, not random.
grounded_facts: An arrow flew toward Yu during the fight.
Yu caught the arrow and remained in the courtyard afterward.
The arrow's arrival coincided with the thief's escape.

memories.memory_text: I asked Sir Te if Governor Yu had ever seen the sword, then suggested the sword could be in his compound.
grounded_facts: Yu enters the great hall while Bo is speaking.
Yu asks Sir Te whether Governor Yu has ever seen the sword.
Yu proposes that the stolen sword could be in Governor Yu's compound.

memories.memory_text: I picked up the flyer after Madam Yu said 'Let me see.'
grounded_facts: Madam Yu said 'Let me see' in response to the posters.
Yu picked up the flyer from the ground after it was discarded.
The governess dismissed the posters as preposterous and tossed the flyer away.

memories.memory_text: After the others went inside, I picked up the torn flyer from the ground and slipped it into my pocket without a word.
grounded_facts: Yu retrieved the discarded flyer after Madam Yu and the governess entered the compound.
Yu studied the flyer and concealed it in her pocket.

memories.memory_text: I saw Jen write my name with such certainty, and when I looked at the characters, I realized they resembled 'sword'—it struck me how closely calligraphy mirrors the precision of fencing.
grounded_facts: Jen writes Yu's name with great confidence and swiftness.
Yu observes that her name in calligraphy looks like the character for 'sword'.
Yu comments that calligraphy is similar to fencing.

memories.memory_text: I told Jen about Meng Si Zhao—how he was Li Mu Bai's sworn brother, how he died, and how I've never known the freedom she longs for, though I desire it just as deeply.
grounded_facts: Yu reveals she was once engaged to Meng Si Zhao.
Meng Si Zhao was killed in battle by Li Mu Bai's enemy.
Yu explains that her feelings for Li Mu Bai grew after Meng's death, but they could not act on them out of respect for Meng's memory.
Yu states she has never tasted the freedom Jen speaks of.

memories.memory_text: I paused in the garden, sensing something off, and then caught the edge of a face—just a profile—watching me from around the corner.
grounded_facts: Yu was escorted out by a maid.
Yu paused and looked around.
Yu saw the Governess's profile peering from around a corner.

memories.memory_text: I remember the dry air pulling at my skin, how I couldn't stand this barren place—no grace, no order—just dust and silence. I asked Jen if she was listening, but she kept staring out, lost in the mountains, as if I weren't even there.
grounded_facts: Yu is in a carriage traveling through the Mongolian desert.
Yu expresses disdain for the desert environment, calling it a 'godforsaken place'.
Yu questions whether Jen is listening to her.
Jen is looking out of the carriage at a mountainside and does not respond to Yu.

memories.memory_text: I saw him turn around, and for a moment, the weight of years fell back onto my shoulders — Li Mu Bai was standing there, unchanged, yet everything between us had shifted.
grounded_facts: Yu entered Sir Te's study and saw a man with his back to her.
Sir Te announced Li Mu Bai's presence before the man turned around.
Li Mu Bai is someone Yu clearly recognizes with emotional significance upon seeing him.

memories.memory_text: I suggested inviting Madam Yu and her daughter, knowing Fox would never ignore a threat to her kin—though Sir Te and Li didn’t understand at first.
grounded_facts: Yu proposed inviting Madam Yu and her daughter to lure Fox.
Yu referenced the strategy 'The best way to trap a fox is through her cubs.'
Sir Te and Li appeared puzzled after Yu's suggestion.

memories.memory_text: I stood in the doorway and asked Li if he was happy the sword was back, feeling the weight of what its return meant—not just for him, but for all of us.
grounded_facts: Yu confronted Li about the return of the sword in the courtyard.
Yu questioned Li's emotional response to having the sword back.
Yu emphasized that the sword no longer belonged to Li, as it had been given to Sir Te.
Yu expressed concern about Jen's future and the disruption Li's proposal would cause.

memories.memory_text: I told Jen to lower the shade, to stay hidden—but then everything went black. I remember the sound of arrows, the guards shouting, and then nothing.
grounded_facts: Yu instructed Jen to lower the carriage shade to avoid being seen by the bandits.
Yu fainted during the attack despite not being physically harmed.
The attack involved arrows, swords, and the slaughter of the guards.

memories.memory_text: I saw Li Mu Bai's fingers at Lo's neck and ran straight at them—I shoved him away and told Lo to come with me, now.
grounded_facts: Yu sees Lo and Li Mu Bai confronting each other on the street.
Li Mu Bai has his fingers pressed against Lo's neck.
Yu physically intervenes by pushing Li Mu Bai away.
Yu speaks directly to Lo, saying 'Come with me! Hurry!'

memories.memory_text: I stood silent as Te opened the empty sword case, anger flaring in his voice, but my focus narrowed when Bo entered and announced Jen had run away—Governor Yu wanted Sir Te’s help to find her.
grounded_facts: Yu was present in Te's hall when Te opened the empty sword case.
Bo entered and reported that Jen had escaped and the wedding chamber was found empty.
Governor Yu requested Sir Te's assistance in finding Jen, citing his knowledge of the Giang Hu underworld.

memories.memory_text: I passed him the tea cup, and our fingers touched. He pulled back, embarrassed, and spoke of impermanence.
grounded_facts: Yu passed a tea cup to Li.
Their fingers touched during the exchange.
Li pulled back, appearing embarrassed.
Li spoke about impermanence after the touch.

memories.memory_text: He took my hand and pressed it to his face. He said my hand was rough from practice, and that he’d never had the courage to touch it. He said he wanted to be with me, just like this, and that it gave him peace.
grounded_facts: Li took Yu's hand and pressed it to his face.
Li remarked that Yu's hand was rough and callused from machete practice.
Li said he had never had the courage to touch Yu's hand before.
Li expressed his desire to be with Yu in that moment.
Li said being with Yu like that gave him a sense of peace.

memories.memory_text: I lit the incense for Meng, feeling the weight of the moment as the smoke curled upward in silence.
grounded_facts: Yu lit incense for her former fiancé Meng in the Hall of Ancestral Worship.
The act was quiet and private, suggesting a moment of personal remembrance.

memories.memory_text: I turned at a sound and saw Jen—dishevelled, standing downstairs, calling out as if returning from the dead.
grounded_facts: Yu heard something and turned to see Jen standing outside downstairs in a dishevelled state.
Jen spoke, addressing Yu as 'Sister Shu Lien,' marking her sudden reappearance.

memories.memory_text: Jen broke down on my shoulder, calling me sister. I told her to look at the trouble she had caused.
grounded_facts: Jen broke down on Yu's shoulder
Jen called Yu 'sister'
Yu told Jen to look at the trouble she had caused

memories.memory_text: I told her I knew she stole the sword—my voice sharp with betrayal—and said she was no sister of mine. When she stood to leave, I blocked her path and demanded she put the sword down.
grounded_facts: Yu accused Jen of stealing the sword
Yu declared Jen was not her sister
Yu intercepted Jen as she tried to leave
Yu commanded Jen to put the sword down

memories.memory_text: I saw the needle in Li Mu Bai's neck and realized Jade Fox had poisoned him—my voice rose, demanding she speak the antidote before she died.
grounded_facts: Yu saw Li Mu Bai pull a needle from his neck
Yu exclaimed 'A poisoned needle!'
Yu urgently demanded Jade Fox reveal the antidote while she was dying

memories.memory_text: I pulled my comb from my hair and gave it to Jen, telling her to take my horse and go to the compound—Mrs. Wu would recognize it and help her prepare the antidote.
grounded_facts: Yu removed her hair comb
Yu gave the comb to Jen as a token
Yu instructed Jen to take her horse and go to the compound to see Mrs. Wu
